violations.description: General food protection. — --Potentially hazardous food was not stored at proper temperature of 41 degrees. Raw chicken in the stand up cooler on the cook-line was temping at 45-47 degrees.

Preventing contamination of packaged and unpackaged food. — --Raw animal food was not properly separated from raw or cooked ready-to-eat food. Raw chicken was stored above raw cut potatoes in the stand-up cooler on the cook-line.

Refrigerated Storage. — --Refrigerator or cold storage was insufficient or ineffective at maintaining potentially hazardous food at a temperature of 41F or less. Stand-up cooler on the cook-line was temping at 45-47 degrees, while holding raw chicken and raw beef.

Date marking and disposition of ready-to-eat potentially hazardous food. — --Refrigerated, ready-to-eat potentially hazardous food was not consumed/discarded within 10 calendar days from the date of preparation. Sauce containers in the reach-in-cooler had dates that were beyond the 10 day limit.
Ventilation hood systems - Design and construction. — --Ventilation hood system was not designed and/or maintained properly. Hood has missing vents over kitchen equipment.

Cleaning frequency of equipment and utensils. — --Nonfood contact surface was not cleaned as frequently as required. Bottom shelving on the cook-line, underneath the flat top, and inside of the microwave need to be cleaned.

Mechanical cleaning and sanitizing requirements. — --Sanitize solution for mechanical warewashing machine was not maintained at the proper concentration. The bar dish machine did not measure for any sanitizer.
Plumbing system. — --Plumbing system was not designed, constructed, operated and/or maintained according to regulation. Hand sink hand a continuous leak at the time of inspection.

Handwashing lavatories. — --Handwashing lavatory was not provided in sufficient number and/or not accessible to allow convenient use by employees. Hand wash sink contained dish washing utensils at the time of inspection and was not accessible for use.

Cleaning and maintenance of floors, walls and ceilings. — --Physical facilities (e.g. floors, walls, ceiling etc…) were not clean or not cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. Floors, walls, and ceilings throughout the facility need to be cleaned more thoroughly.

Lighting. — --Lighting did not have protective shielding in areas where there is exposed food, clean equipment/utensils, or unwrapped single-service items. Exposed glass lighting over clean dishes and utensil rack in the kitchen.

Storage and handling of maintenance items. — --Wet-use maintenance items, such as mops, were not placed in a position that allows proper air-drying. Wet mops stored on the floor in the mop sink area and were not placed in a position to properly dry. Need to be hung up where they can get proper airflow.

Handling and storage of poisonous and toxic materials. — --Secondary container of poisonous or toxic material was not clearly identified to indicated contents. Unlabeled chemical bottle in the mop sink area.
